purpose: Domain packs are the reusable scaffolding that authors and reviewers draw from when writing or uplifting NIS2 UC sidecars. Each pack pre-populates the data-source family's TA, sourcetypes, fields, CIM mapping, validation pattern, KFPs, and troubleshooting modes so sibling UCs read coherently. The pack is reference material — it is NOT auto-merged into UC sidecars; authors copy and customise.

ta.name: Splunk Enterprise Security
ta.scope: Premium app on a dedicated Search Head or Search Head Cluster; not installed on indexers or forwarders.
ta.license: ES is a premium app. Notable storage uses the KV-store-backed `incident_review` collection from ES 7.0 onwards; older ES used `index=notable`.

data.expansion: Defined in `SA-Threatintelligence/macros.conf`. Resolves to a search over the `notable` index (or KV-store collection in ES 7.0+) with `eventtype=notable`.
data.expectedVolume: 1 row per notable raised. Fleet steady-state typically 50–500 notables/day depending on correlation-rule scope and tuning.

cim.dmaRecommendation: Alerts data model is small; acceleration adds little value. Run searches against the macro directly.

pages: Configure > Incident Management > Notable Event Suppressions
spotChecks: Pick two notables in Incident Review; confirm `rule_name`, `urgency`, `status` match the macro output.
Run `\`notable\` | timechart count` over 24h and confirm against ES Incident Review queue depth.
Run `\`notable\` | stats count by status` and reconcile with the Status filter in Incident Review.

kfps.scenario: Active major incident — analysts deliberately leave notables open while triaging the underlying campaign.
kfps.distinguish: Look for a parent risk object with `risk_score > 250` in `index=risk` for the same time window, and an open ServiceNow MAJ ticket.
kfps.suppress: Mark the parent campaign as a single notable suppression in `Configure > Incident Management > Notable Event Suppressions` for the duration of the incident; do not suppress the rule globally.

kfps.scenario: Holiday weekend or staffing gap — no analyst on shift to acknowledge.
kfps.distinguish: Cross-check `index=pagerduty sourcetype=pagerduty:incident` for an unacknowledged page corresponding to the notable.
kfps.suppress: Do NOT suppress; instead escalate the on-call schedule. The aged notable is a real finding for the IR drill metric.

kfps.scenario: Bulk recategorisation after correlation-rule tuning — notables are temporarily not closed while analysts re-triage.
kfps.distinguish: Correlate with the `lastModified` timestamp on the correlation search (`| rest /servicesNS/-/SA-ThreatIntelligence/saved/searches`).
kfps.suppress: Time-bound exception in `nis2_exception_register.csv` keyed on `rule_name`, valid for the 7-day re-triage window.

kfps.scenario: Risk-Based Alerting (RBA) suppresses individual notables — only the aggregate fires as a notable.
kfps.distinguish: If a per-rule notable is missing but `index=risk` shows recent contributions for that rule, RBA is consolidating.
kfps.suppress: Do not flag — the aggregate is the correct evidence surface. Update the UC's SPL to count risk-aggregated notables instead.

kfps.scenario: Notable raised by a correlation search whose `urgency` is set to `informational` — collected for context, not paged.
kfps.distinguish: `\`notable\` urgency="informational"` should NOT count toward the SLA metric.
kfps.suppress: Filter `urgency IN ("high","critical")` in the SPL, as the exemplars do.

troubleshooting.cause: ES is not installed on the search head OR the user lacks `read` on `index=notable`.
troubleshooting.fix: Confirm with `| rest /services/apps/local | search title=SplunkEnterpriseSecuritySuite | table version`. Add `read` for `index=notable` to the user's role.

troubleshooting.cause: Risk-Based Alerting is consolidating individual rule fires; the only notables raised are aggregate `Risk Analysis` notables which default to `informational`.
troubleshooting.fix: Either pivot to `index=risk` for evidence, or raise the urgency on the aggregate Risk Analysis correlation search via `Configure > Content Management > Risk Analysis`.

troubleshooting.cause: Notables raised before ES 7.0 used `index=notable` and an `event_id`-keyed adhoc lookup for status; after 7.0 the source of truth is the KV-store `incident_review` collection. The macro reconciles, but lookup permissions must be granted.
troubleshooting.fix: Confirm role has `read` on the `incident_review` KV collection: `| inputlookup incident_review_lookup | head 1` should return a row.

troubleshooting.symptom: Notable counts in Splunk match Incident Review but do not match SOAR/ServiceNow ticket counts.
troubleshooting.cause: Not every notable opens a ticket — only those above the SOAR playbook threshold. Or the ITSM forwarder is dropping events.
troubleshooting.fix: Reconcile against the SOAR playbook configuration (which urgencies trigger ticket creation). For ITSM, check `index=_internal source=*soar* OR source=*snow*` for HEC errors.

troubleshooting.cause: Forwarder clock skew on the indexer that originally indexed the notable, or `_time` was overridden to a parsed event timestamp instead of the notable creation time.
troubleshooting.fix: Check `\`notable\` | eval lag=_indextime-_time | stats avg(lag) p95(lag) max(lag)`. Investigate hosts with large lag via `chronyc sources`.

troubleshooting.symptom: After upgrading ES, the macro returns rows but `rule_name` is empty.
troubleshooting.cause: ES 7.3+ moved the rule_name lookup to a JSON config in the KV store. The macro expansion in older saved searches still references the legacy CSV.
troubleshooting.fix: Re-import the macro from the current `SA-Threatintelligence` app or run `splunk reload deploy-server` if cluster-managed. Verify with `| rest /services/saved/searches/notable | table search`.

ta.name: Splunk SOAR (formerly Phantom)
ta.scope: Splunk SOAR is a separate platform. The Splunk Add-on for Phantom (Splunkbase 3411) ships SOAR's audit and case data into Splunk for searching.
ta.license: Splunk SOAR is licensed by case volume; the add-on is free. The Phantom Add-on requires API token (read-only) on the SOAR instance.

data.expectedVolume: 1 container per SOC ticket-equivalent. Fleet 100–1000 containers/day depending on SOAR scope.

cim.dmaRecommendation: Phantom add-on tags actions to Change for audit. Acceleration is optional; the data is small.

spotChecks: Pick a recent container in SOAR; verify `container_id`, `status`, `owner`, `due_time` in Splunk match.
`index=soar sourcetype=phantom:container | timechart count` should match the SOAR > Investigations queue depth.

kfps.scenario: SOAR playbook re-runs after a code change — duplicate `action_run` records for the same container.
kfps.distinguish: Group by `(container_id, playbook_name)` and take `latest(_time)` to dedupe.
kfps.suppress: SPL: `| dedup container_id, playbook_name`

kfps.scenario: Containers stuck in `New` because the auto-assignment playbook is disabled during maintenance.
kfps.distinguish: Check the auto-assignment playbook's `last_run` time; if older than the container's `create_time`, the playbook is the cause.
kfps.suppress: Time-bound exception keyed on `label` for the maintenance period.

kfps.scenario: Closed containers re-opened after analyst review — `status` flips from `Closed` back to `Open`.
kfps.distinguish: Look for `phantom:audit` events with `action=container_status_change` and `new_value=Open` on the container.
kfps.suppress: Reset the SLA clock from the most recent re-open time, not from the original create time.

kfps.scenario: External integration timeout (e.g. ServiceNow API down) leaves `action_run` in `Pending` — not a real backlog.
kfps.distinguish: Filter to `status NOT IN ("Pending", "Running")` for the SLA metric; track Pending separately.
kfps.suppress: Update the SPL `where` clause.

troubleshooting.cause: Phantom add-on not configured or API token expired.
troubleshooting.fix: On the SOAR HF, check `index=_internal source=*phantom* | head 20` for OAuth/HTTP errors. Refresh the API token in Phantom Add-on Settings.

troubleshooting.cause: SOAR < 6.0 stored the name only in `meta`; the add-on has been updated to extract from `meta.name`.
troubleshooting.fix: Update the Phantom Add-on to ≥3.0; older versions miss recent fields.

troubleshooting.symptom: Counts don't match the SOAR UI.
troubleshooting.cause: Multi-tenant SOAR — the API token has access to one tenant only.
troubleshooting.fix: Enumerate tenants in SOAR Settings; create a token per tenant or use a service-account with cross-tenant scope.

ta.name: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow
ta.scope: Heavy Forwarder with reachability to the ServiceNow instance API. Requires REST OAuth or basic-auth credentials with `read` on the GRC tables.
ta.license: Free. ServiceNow GRC plugins (Policy & Compliance, Risk, Audit) are licensed separately by ServiceNow.

data.expectedVolume: 1 record per row per change. GRC tables typically have hundreds to low thousands of records; full sync ≈ 1 MB/poll.

cim.dmaRecommendation: Acceleration recommended for `Change` if multiple ITSM-feeding UCs are in flight.

validation.vendorUi: ServiceNow > GRC > Policy and Compliance > Policies; > Risk > Risk Register; > Audit > Audit Tasks
spotChecks: Pick a known policy in ServiceNow GRC; verify the `sys_id` and `state` match in Splunk.
Compare the Splunk row count to the `Number of Records` shown in the ServiceNow table list view.

kfps.scenario: Quarterly attestation push — the modular input ingests hundreds of policy/risk records that look 'updated'.
kfps.distinguish: The `sys_updated_on` for the bulk batch falls within the attestation window (typically the first business day of the quarter).
kfps.suppress: Time-bound exception in `nis2_exception_register.csv` for the attestation window; apply `where NOT (date_mday IN (1,2) AND date_month IN ("january","april","july","october"))` only as a last resort.

kfps.scenario: Policy retirement — a policy is intentionally moved to `state=Retired` and an `evidence_age_days` calculation flags it as overdue.
kfps.distinguish: Filter `state` IN (active states) before the SLA calculation: `| where state IN ("Published", "Approved", "Under Review")`.
kfps.suppress: Update the SPL filter; do not suppress the rule.

kfps.scenario: Risk register bulk re-scoring — the risk owner changes the `risk_score` for many rows in one shift.
kfps.distinguish: Look for many `sys_updated_on` entries for one `sys_updated_by` user in a tight window.
kfps.suppress: Compare against the change request that authorised the re-scoring; do not suppress in SPL.

kfps.scenario: Audit task closed but the closure note hasn't propagated — the task shows `state=Closed Complete` but `actual_end` is null.
kfps.distinguish: Wait one polling cycle (5 min) and re-evaluate; if still null, the audit lead didn't fill in `actual_end`.
kfps.suppress: Notify the audit lead via runbook; do not auto-suppress.

troubleshooting.cause: The modular input is configured for a different table or the ServiceNow GRC plugin is not licensed.
troubleshooting.fix: On the HF, `splunk btool inputs list --debug | grep servicenow_modular_input`. Confirm the stanza names `sn_grc_policy` (or `sn_compliance_policy` depending on plugin).

troubleshooting.cause: OAuth token expired or service account locked.
troubleshooting.fix: Refresh the OAuth token in ServiceNow Add-on Settings; verify the service-account in ServiceNow > User Administration > Users is not locked.

troubleshooting.cause: ServiceNow REST rate limit hit.
troubleshooting.fix: Increase `interval` on the modular input; raise the ServiceNow rate-limit profile via ServiceNow Admin > System Properties > glide.rest.outbound.max.

troubleshooting.symptom: Splunk records older than the corresponding ServiceNow records.
troubleshooting.cause: ServiceNow timezone mismatch — modular input timestamps in UTC but the table stores in instance TZ.
troubleshooting.fix: Set the modular input's `timezone` to UTC and reconcile with the ServiceNow `glide.sys.default.tz` system property.

ta.name: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (CMDB tables) OR Splunk Asset & Identity framework (ES) OR Splunk DB Connect
ta.scope: Same HF as servicenow-grc when sourcing from ServiceNow CMDB. ES Asset & Identity framework runs on the ES SH and reads from KV-store / lookup files.
ta.license: ServiceNow CMDB is part of ITSM. The ES Asset & Identity framework is included with ES.

data.expectedVolume: 1 row per CI per change. CMDB sizes range 5k–500k CIs depending on org.

cim.dmaRecommendation: Asset & Identity framework is mandatory for ES correlation searches that pivot on asset criticality.

validation.vendorUi: ServiceNow > Configuration > CIs > All Configuration Items
spotChecks: Pick a known production server CI; confirm `name`, `ip_address`, `support_group` match in Splunk.
Run `| inputlookup assets_by_str.csv | stats dc(asset)` and reconcile with ServiceNow's CMDB > All CIs filter for the same scope.

kfps.scenario: RMA / hardware refresh — asset is decommissioned in CMDB but still appears in monitoring data because forwarder hasn't been removed.
kfps.distinguish: Look for `operational_status=6` (Retired). Compare to recent `_indextime` for the host.
kfps.suppress: Filter `where operational_status != 6` in the SPL; add a runbook step to remove the forwarder.

kfps.scenario: Newly onboarded business unit — CIs created without `u_nis2_in_scope` populated.
kfps.distinguish: `u_nis2_in_scope` is null AND `sys_created_on > now()-7d`.
kfps.suppress: Defer the SLA clock by 7 days from creation; surface to the asset owner for classification.

kfps.scenario: Bulk import from network discovery (e.g. ServiceNow Discovery, Cisco Catalyst Center) creates duplicate CIs with different `name` formats.
kfps.distinguish: Group by `serial_number` or `mac_address`; if multiple `sys_id` map to one device, you have duplicates.
kfps.suppress: Run the ServiceNow CMDB reconciliation rule; do not suppress in SPL.

kfps.scenario: Cloud auto-scaling group — short-lived CIs created and destroyed, never reach steady-state in CMDB.
kfps.distinguish: `ci_class` is `cmdb_ci_aws_ec2_instance` AND `lifecycle_seconds < 3600`.
kfps.suppress: Exclude transient CIs from the scope SLA; track them via cloud-native inventory instead.

troubleshooting.symptom: Asset lookup returns 0 rows in ES.
troubleshooting.cause: ES Asset & Identity inputs are not configured to merge the ServiceNow CMDB feed.
troubleshooting.fix: ES > Configure > Data Enrichment > Asset and Identity Configuration > Asset Sources. Add the `assets_by_str` lookup; click `Save & Apply`.

troubleshooting.symptom: `u_nis2_in_scope` field is null on every CI.
troubleshooting.cause: The custom field exists in CMDB but isn't included in the modular input's `field_list` filter.
troubleshooting.fix: Update `inputs.conf` for the modular input to add `field_list=...,u_nis2_in_scope,u_sector,u_nis2_essential_or_important`.

troubleshooting.cause: Discovery sources are creating CIs without reconciliation; duplicates inflate the count.
troubleshooting.fix: ServiceNow CMDB > Reconciliation > Identification Rules. Configure SerialNumber or MacAddress as the identity attribute.

ta.name: Veeam App for Splunk (or Commvault, Rubrik, Cohesity, AWS Backup, Druva equivalents)
ta.scope: TA installed on a HF that can reach the backup product's API or syslog. Veeam supports SNMP traps + Veeam REST + raw event log.
ta.license: TA is free. Veeam (and other backup) licensing is per-protected-VM/TB.

data.expectedVolume: 1 event per job execution per target. Fleet 1k–10k jobs/day.

cim.dmaRecommendation: No native CIM mapping for backup. Build domain dashboards directly.

validation.vendorUi: Veeam Backup & Replication Console > Statistics; Commvault Command Center > Job Controller; Rubrik UI > Events; Cohesity Helios > Audit Log
spotChecks: Pick a job that just ran; confirm `start_time`, `end_time`, `result` in Splunk match the Veeam Console.
Run `| stats latest(_time) by job_name` and reconcile with the Veeam job list.

kfps.scenario: Tape rotation maintenance window — backups deliberately offlined for ~2h while libraries are serviced.
kfps.distinguish: Match the gap to the `index=changes` CHG record for tape maintenance; the gap is bounded.
kfps.suppress: Time-bound exception in `nis2_exception_register.csv` keyed on `target_system` for the window.

kfps.scenario: Deduplication catalogue rebuild after a Veeam upgrade — first post-upgrade backup runs longer and may hit warning state.
kfps.distinguish: Look for the upgrade event `index=_internal source=*veeam* upgrade` within 24h of the warning.
kfps.suppress: Treat as informational for 24h after upgrade; surface as a finding only after the second backup also warns.

kfps.scenario: Newly added job — first run is a full backup, takes longer, may overlap the next scheduled job.
kfps.system: Veeam > Jobs > new job's run history
kfps.distinguish: `start_time` of the second run intersects `end_time` of the first; both same `job_name` with `is_full=1` for the first.
kfps.suppress: Allow 3 successful runs before applying the SLA; use `| where job_run_seq > 3`.

kfps.scenario: Backup target is full — Veeam reports `Warning` not `Failed`, but the next run will fail unless storage is freed.
kfps.distinguish: Look for `state=Warning` AND repository_free_pct < 10.
kfps.suppress: Do not suppress; this is a real finding. Page the storage team.

troubleshooting.cause: Veeam syslog forwarding not enabled, or Veeam REST API token expired.
troubleshooting.fix: Veeam Console > General Options > Notifications > SNMP/Syslog. For REST, regenerate the API token in Veeam Console > Users.

troubleshooting.symptom: `status` field is empty on most events.
troubleshooting.cause: Veeam JSON event format changed in v12.1; older props.conf extractions miss the new path.
troubleshooting.fix: Upgrade the Veeam App for Splunk to the latest; props.conf in the app handles both pre-v12 and v12.1+.

troubleshooting.symptom: Duplicate events for the same job.
troubleshooting.cause: Both syslog AND the API are configured; Veeam emits twice.
troubleshooting.fix: Disable syslog OR disable the API input; pick one canonical path.

troubleshooting.cause: SureBackup / SureReplica jobs are a separate event class; the modular input may not include them.
troubleshooting.fix: Check `index=backup sourcetype=veeam:event eventtype=*surebackup*`. Update the input's filter to include surebackup events.

ta.name: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Azure / Okta / Microsoft 365
ta.scope: HF with reachability to Microsoft Graph and/or Okta API. OAuth client credentials with `AuditLog.Read.All` (Azure) or `okta.logs.read` (Okta).
ta.license: Free. Azure AD P1/P2 license required for risk events; Okta API rate limits depend on org size.
siblingTAs: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (Splunkbase 3110)
Splunk Add-on for Okta Identity Cloud (Splunkbase 6553)

data.expectedVolume: 1 event per sign-in. Fleet 100k–10M events/day for large orgs.

cim.dmaRecommendation: Authentication DMA is mandatory for fleet-scale MFA queries — raw queries time out beyond ~1M events.

validation.vendorUi: Microsoft Entra > Sign-in logs; Okta > Reports > System Log
pages: Entra > Conditional Access > Insights and reporting
spotChecks: Find your own most recent sign-in in Entra; verify `userPrincipalName`, `authenticationMethod`, `conditionalAccessStatus` in Splunk match.
Run `| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action` and reconcile with Entra > Sign-ins > Filter by Status.

kfps.scenario: Conditional Access policy blocks legacy auth — generates `result=Failure` but is the desired enforcement, not a security event.
kfps.distinguish: `result=Failure` AND `result_reason` matches `legacy authentication blocked`.
kfps.suppress: Filter the SPL to exclude `result_reason=*legacy*`; track legacy-blocked separately for compliance evidence.

kfps.scenario: MFA fatigue protection (Microsoft Authenticator number-matching) — generates `Failed` events when the user types the wrong number, but the user is legitimate.
kfps.distinguish: `authenticationDetails.authenticationStepResultDetail` includes `Wrong number`. The user retries successfully within 60s.
kfps.suppress: Group by `(user, ipAddress)` over 5min; if any success follows the failure, treat as one successful event.

kfps.scenario: Service account using legacy SAML for B2B integration — `authenticationRequirement` is `singleFactorAuthentication` by design.
kfps.distinguish: `appId` in `nis2_b2b_apps_allowlist.csv`.
kfps.suppress: `| lookup nis2_b2b_apps_allowlist.csv appId OUTPUT b2b_allowed | where isnull(b2b_allowed)`

kfps.scenario: Break-glass account — single-factor by design, monitored separately for use.
kfps.system: Entra > Conditional Access policy excluding break-glass account
kfps.distinguish: `userPrincipalName` matches a known break-glass UPN (held in `nis2_breakglass_accounts.csv`).
kfps.suppress: Filter break-glass from the MFA % calculation; monitor `count` of break-glass uses separately and alert on any.

kfps.scenario: Recently provisioned user — first 24h of sign-ins may use SMS while waiting for hardware-token provisioning.
kfps.distinguish: User's `createdDateTime` (Entra) within last 24h.
kfps.suppress: Defer the MFA-strength SLA by 24h from createdDateTime.

troubleshooting.cause: App registration permissions wrong, or Azure AD diagnostic settings not exporting to the Event Hub the TA reads from.
troubleshooting.fix: Confirm in Azure Portal > Microsoft Entra ID > Diagnostic settings that `SignInLogs` is exported. Verify the TA's app registration has `AuditLog.Read.All` consented at the tenant level.

troubleshooting.symptom: `mfa_used` always 0 even when MFA is enforced.
troubleshooting.cause: TA version < 5.0 doesn't extract `authenticationDetails`; the SPL's regex on `_raw` falls through.
troubleshooting.fix: Upgrade the TA. Verify by running `index=azure sourcetype=ms:aad:signin | head 1 | table authenticationDetails`.

troubleshooting.symptom: Okta event count is 1/10 of Okta's UI count.
troubleshooting.cause: Okta API rate-limit dropped events, or the modular input's `start_time` advanced past unfetched events.
troubleshooting.fix: Check `index=_internal source=*okta* | search 429`. Reset the Okta input's checkpoint via `splunk reset-input`.

troubleshooting.cause: TA's eventtypes.conf doesn't tag the events with `authentication`; DMA acceleration relies on the tag.
troubleshooting.fix: On the SH, `| eventcount summarize=false index=azure | head 1` and confirm `eventtype=ms_aad_signin` and `tag::eventtype=authentication`.

ta.name: Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (or BeyondTrust, HashiCorp Vault equivalents)
ta.scope: Syslog from CyberArk PVWA / EPV. The TA parses CEF/Syslog. For Vault: HCP/Enterprise audit log ingest via HEC.
ta.license: Free. CyberArk PAM, BeyondTrust Password Safe, and Vault Enterprise are licensed by user/secret count.

data.expectedVolume: 1 event per password retrieval / session start. Fleet 1k–50k events/day.

cim.dmaRecommendation: Authentication acceleration; Change acceleration if ITSM correlation is required.

validation.vendorUi: CyberArk PVWA > Reports > Activity; BeyondTrust > Reports > User Access Activity
pages: CyberArk > Master Policy > Privileged Access Workflows
spotChecks: Pull a recent `Show Password` action from PVWA Reports; confirm `actor`, `vault_account`, `_time` match.
Compare `| stats count by actor` to the Activity report for the same scope.

kfps.scenario: Scheduled service-account password rotation — generates `password change` events that look like privileged activity.
kfps.system: CyberArk > Master Policy > Password Management > Rotation
kfps.distinguish: `actor=Master Policy Engine` (or service-account name); `command=ChangePassword`.
kfps.suppress: Exclude the rotation engine actor from the SLA; track separately for change-management evidence.

kfps.scenario: Approved emergency access — out-of-hours retrieval with multi-step approval chain.
kfps.distinguish: `approval_chain` is non-empty AND each approval is from a distinct `approver_id`.
kfps.suppress: Cross-reference with `index=changes` for an emergency CHG record; exclude only when matched.

kfps.scenario: Discovery scan — CyberArk's account-discovery service touches every safe.
kfps.distinguish: `actor=PasswordManager` AND `command=Discover`.
kfps.suppress: Exclude the `PasswordManager` actor from the activity SLA.

troubleshooting.cause: CyberArk PVWA syslog forwarding not enabled, or syslog destination IP wrong.
troubleshooting.fix: PVWA > Administration > Configuration Options > Logging > Syslog. Confirm destination matches the SC4S / HF receiver.

troubleshooting.cause: Session was started but PSM (Privileged Session Manager) didn't emit the close event because the session is still open.
troubleshooting.fix: Filter `| where outcome="Closed" AND session_duration > 0` for SLA calculations.

troubleshooting.cause: HEC token reached its rate limit, or Vault audit device disabled.
troubleshooting.fix: On Vault: `vault audit list`. On Splunk: check `index=_internal source=*hec* token=*vault*` for 429.

ta.name: Tenable Add-On for Splunk (or Qualys, Rapid7, Defender Vulnerability Management equivalents)
ta.scope: HF with API reachability to Tenable.io / Tenable.sc / Qualys cloud. API key with `Vulnerabilities`/`Reports` scope.
ta.license: Free. Vendor licenses by asset count.

data.expectedVolume: 1 event per vulnerability per asset per scan. Fleet 100k–10M events/day.

cim.dmaRecommendation: Vulnerabilities DMA is mandatory for any fleet-scale query — raw counts time out at ~1M rows.

spotChecks: Pick a known critical CVE on a known asset; confirm `plugin_id`, `severity`, `last_found` in Splunk match.
`| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity` matches Tenable.io > Dashboard severity tiles.

kfps.scenario: Quarterly Patch Tuesday — fresh CVEs published, all assets show `state=open` until next scan completes.
kfps.distinguish: `first_found > now()-7d` AND `patch_published > now()-7d`.
kfps.suppress: Defer the SLA clock by 7 days from `first_found` for newly published CVEs; track separately for trend.

kfps.scenario: Asset reimaged — old vulnerability records persist for ~30 days until Tenable expires them.
kfps.system: Tenable.io > Assets > Asset Details > Last Authenticated Scan
kfps.distinguish: `last_found < now()-30d` AND `state=open`.
kfps.suppress: Filter the SPL to `last_found > now()-30d` for the SLA metric.

kfps.scenario: Compensating control in place — VPR is high but the asset is segmented and unreachable from untrusted zones.
kfps.distinguish: Lookup `nis2_compensating_controls.csv` keyed on `(plugin_id, asset_id)` returns a non-expired entry.
kfps.suppress: Filter the SPL via lookup; require the compensating-control owner to renew quarterly.

kfps.scenario: Vendor false positive (e.g. Tenable plugin 12345 misdetects on CentOS Stream).
kfps.distinguish: Tenable issues a plugin update; the next scan correctly classifies.
kfps.suppress: Time-bound exception keyed on `(plugin_id, distro)` until the plugin update is deployed.

troubleshooting.cause: Tenable.io export job failed (export takes 5–30 min to materialise); API key scope insufficient.
troubleshooting.fix: On Tenable: Settings > My Account > API Keys > regenerate; ensure scope includes `vulns`. Check `index=_internal source=*tenable* | head 20`.

troubleshooting.symptom: Asset count is 1/3 of Tenable.io count.
troubleshooting.cause: Splunk is only ingesting `state=open`; closed/fixed are excluded by the export query.
troubleshooting.fix: If full asset count is needed, change the export query to include `state IN (open, fixed, reopened)`. Default is `open` only.

troubleshooting.cause: TA's tags.conf doesn't tag events as `vulnerability`.
troubleshooting.fix: On the SH, `| eventcount summarize=false index=vulnerability | head 1` should show `eventtype=tenable_io_vuln` and `tag::eventtype=vulnerability`.

troubleshooting.cause: Both agent-based and network-based scans configured; same asset scanned twice.
troubleshooting.fix: Dedupe in SPL on `(plugin_id, asset_uuid)` taking `latest(_time)`.

ta.name: KnowBe4 Add-on for Splunk (or Cornerstone, Workday Learning equivalents)
ta.scope: HF with API reachability to KnowBe4. Reporter API token (read-only).
ta.license: Free. KnowBe4 / Cornerstone / Workday licenses by user.

data.expectedVolume: 1 event per user per training assignment status change. Fleet 50k–500k events/day.

cim.dmaRecommendation: No CIM mapping for LMS. Build domain dashboards directly.

validation.vendorUi: KnowBe4 Console > Training > Campaigns; Cornerstone > Reports > Transcript Status
spotChecks: Pick a recent campaign; verify `completion_status` per user matches the KnowBe4 transcript.
`| stats count by completion_status` reconciles with the campaign summary.

kfps.scenario: Quarterly campaign rollover — bulk `Past Due` ratings resolve over 30 days as users complete.
kfps.distinguish: `due_date` for many users falls in the same week.
kfps.suppress: Defer the SLA clock by 30 days from `due_date` for the rollover quarter.

kfps.scenario: New hire — assigned training but in onboarding window; not yet at desk.
kfps.distinguish: Lookup `hr_employees.csv` for `start_date > now()-14d`.
kfps.suppress: Defer SLA by 30 days from start_date.

kfps.scenario: Leave of absence — user assigned but on parental/medical leave.
kfps.distinguish: Lookup `hr_loa.csv` for `loa_active=true`.
kfps.suppress: Suspend the SLA for the LoA period; resume on return.

kfps.scenario: Phishing test follow-up training — assigned only after a click; due date is 14 days post-click.
kfps.distinguish: `enrollment_id` references a campaign with `is_remediation=true`.
kfps.suppress: Use a separate SLA (14 days) for remediation training; do not group with annual.

troubleshooting.cause: KnowBe4 Reporter API token expired or rate-limited.
troubleshooting.fix: KnowBe4 Console > Account Settings > API > Regenerate Reporter API token. Increase poll `interval` if hitting 429.

troubleshooting.symptom: User counts don't match KnowBe4 active-users.
troubleshooting.cause: Active Directory sync delay — KnowBe4 enrolled users not yet in `hr_employees.csv`.
troubleshooting.fix: Verify the AD sync schedule in KnowBe4 Console > Account Settings > Active Directory.

ta.name: Splunk Stream (TLS metadata) + Venafi + HashiCorp Vault audit
ta.scope: Splunk Stream on a network tap or via SPAN port for TLS handshake metadata. Venafi REST polling on HF. Vault audit via HEC.
ta.license: Splunk Stream is free; Venafi and Vault licensed separately.

data.expectedVolume: 1 event per TLS handshake per session. Fleet 1k–10M events/day depending on tap scope.

cim.dmaRecommendation: Certificates DMA acceleration is recommended for any cert-inventory query.

validation.vendorUi: Venafi TPP > Policy Tree > Certificates; Vault UI > Secrets > PKI
spotChecks: Pick a known certificate in Venafi; confirm `cert_serial`, `cert_expiry` match in Splunk.
Run `| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Certificates.SSL.cert_expiry) by Certificates.SSL.cert_subject_common_name` and reconcile with Venafi inventory.

kfps.scenario: Test environment — self-signed certs, weak ciphers — by design.
kfps.distinguish: `ssl_subject` matches `*-test.*` or `dev.*` patterns.
kfps.suppress: Filter test domains via lookup `nis2_environment_classification.csv`.

kfps.scenario: Legacy IoT device — only supports TLS 1.0 / 1.1; on a segmented VLAN with compensating controls.
kfps.distinguish: `src` IP in the IoT VLAN range AND device is in `nis2_compensating_controls.csv`.
kfps.suppress: Filter via lookup; do not suppress globally.

kfps.scenario: Cipher rotation in progress — old cipher temporarily allowed during a rolling upgrade.
kfps.distinguish: Match a `index=changes` CHG record for cipher migration; bounded time window.
kfps.suppress: Time-bound exception during the migration window.

kfps.scenario: Cert renewed but old cert still in cache — Splunk Stream sees both old and new for ~24h.
kfps.system: Application cert cache (typically Java keystore or nginx)
kfps.distinguish: Two `cert_serial` for same `ssl_subject` within 24h; older serial expires inside 24h.
kfps.suppress: Dedupe to `latest(_time) by ssl_subject, cert_serial`.

troubleshooting.cause: Splunk Stream not deployed or not seeing the tap traffic.
troubleshooting.fix: Splunk Stream > Configuration > Stream Sources > confirm `tls` stream is enabled and `tap_interface` is set.

troubleshooting.cause: Venafi TPP polling job disabled or REST credential lacks `Read` on certificates.
troubleshooting.fix: Venafi > Identity > Permissions > confirm the API account has `Read` on the policy folder.

ta.name: Splunk Stream (DNS) OR Cisco Umbrella TA OR Splunk Add-on for Microsoft DNS / BIND
ta.scope: Splunk Stream on the recursive resolver path, OR ingest from Microsoft DNS Analytical Channel via Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows.
ta.license: Free. Volume can be 100GB+/day at large orgs.

data.expectedVolume: 1 event per DNS query. Fleet 10M–1B events/day.

cim.dmaRecommendation: Mandatory acceleration; raw queries time out beyond seconds.

validation.vendorUi: Cisco Umbrella > Reports > Activity Search; Microsoft DNS Console > Event Viewer
pages: Splunk Stream > Configuration > Stream Sources > DNS
spotChecks: Issue a DNS query for a known domain; verify `query`, `answer` in Splunk within 30 seconds.
`| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Resolution.DNS by DNS.query` top-N matches Umbrella's top queries.

kfps.scenario: Software update poll — Microsoft / Adobe / vendor SaaS makes 1000s of repetitive DNS queries to update servers.
kfps.distinguish: `domain` matches a known update domain (e.g. `windowsupdate.com`).
kfps.suppress: Filter via `nis2_dns_update_allowlist.csv`.

kfps.scenario: DNS cache flush — temporary spike in queries after a TTL expiry.
kfps.distinguish: Bounded spike; returns to baseline in 5 min.
kfps.suppress: Use a 5-min smoothed average for the SLA.

troubleshooting.cause: Splunk Stream not deployed, or Microsoft DNS Analytical Channel not enabled.
troubleshooting.fix: Splunk Stream > confirm DNS stream is enabled and unfiltered. For MS DNS, enable the Analytical channel via `wevtutil sl Microsoft-Windows-DNSServer/Analytical /e:true /q:true`.

ta.name: Cisco Cyber Vision OR Nozomi Networks OR Claroty OR Splunk OT Security Add-on + Splunk Edge Hub
ta.scope: Cyber Vision Center has a Splunk forwarder integration; Nozomi/Claroty syslog. Edge Hub for direct OT sensor ingest.
ta.license: OT Security Add-on requires Splunk OT Security; sensor TAs licensed by vendor.

data.expectedVolume: 1 event per asset state change or detected anomaly. Fleet ~10–100k events/day per OT site.

cim.dmaRecommendation: OT data is sparse; acceleration optional.

validation.vendorUi: Cyber Vision Center > Inventory > Components; Nozomi Guardian > Asset Management
spotChecks: Pick a known PLC; verify `asset_id`, `vendor`, `model` in Splunk match.
`| stats dc(asset_id)` matches Cyber Vision Inventory count for the same site.

kfps.scenario: Scheduled engineering download — programmer connects to PLC during a maintenance window; looks like privileged access.
kfps.distinguish: Match against `index=changes` for a CHG record; engineering laptop in `nis2_ot_engineer_endpoints.csv`.
kfps.suppress: Time-bound exception during the work permit window.

kfps.scenario: Sensor discovery scan — Nozomi/Claroty active scan triggers protocol traffic that looks anomalous.
kfps.distinguish: `src_ip` matches the Guardian's IP range.
kfps.suppress: Filter the Guardian's IP from the anomaly SLA.

kfps.scenario: Process startup — first 10 minutes of a process run produce setpoint changes that look like tampering.
kfps.distinguish: Process is in `Startup` mode in the historian.
kfps.suppress: Suspend the SLA for 10 min after the historian's `Startup` flag is set.

troubleshooting.cause: Cyber Vision sensor not paired with the Splunk integration, or syslog destination wrong.
troubleshooting.fix: Cyber Vision Center > Settings > Splunk Integration > confirm receiver IP.

troubleshooting.cause: Cyber Vision exports a different asset reference key in newer firmware.
troubleshooting.fix: Update the Cyber Vision TA to the latest; confirm the `asset_id` extraction in the TA's `props.conf`.

ta.name: Splunk-internal evidence index — author-managed
ta.scope: Index `audit_evidence` is a restricted index used to archive UC outputs, dashboard exports, and saved-search results for tamper-evident retention. Roles: only `audit_evidence_writer` can write; `audit_evidence_reader` can read.
ta.license: Standard Splunk indexing licence applies. RFC 3161 timestamping (`evidenceSigning.method = rfc3161-tsa`) is recommended for production.

data.expectedVolume: 1 event per UC saved-search run. Steady-state ~100MB/day for the full Tier-1 catalogue.

cim.dmaRecommendation: None — evidence index is not a CIM source.

validation.vendorUi: Splunk audit log (`index=_audit`) — the canonical source-of-truth for who-ran-what
spotChecks: `index=audit_evidence | stats count by uc_id` shows expected entries for each scheduled UC.
Check `index=_audit action=search uc_id=*` for the corresponding search executions.

kfps.scenario: Scheduled UC search produces 0 results — no evidence event written.
kfps.distinguish: Result count = 0 is itself evidence; ensure the saved search writes a `result_count=0` event to `audit_evidence`.
kfps.suppress: Configure the saved search's alert action to write to `audit_evidence` even on 0 results.

troubleshooting.cause: Saved-search alert action not configured to summary-index into `audit_evidence`.
troubleshooting.fix: Edit the saved search > Edit Alert Actions > Add `Summary Index = audit_evidence`.
